frame: Legal System Weaponisation Narrative: Women Who File DV and Dowry Cases Are Liars
generated_comment: Main kuch nahi bolunga kisi ke baare mein... par itna zaroor kehna chahungi — ghar ki baat ghar mein rehni chahiye. Mujhe bhi takleef thi, bahut takleef thi, lekin kya main court gayi? Kya maine apne pati ka naam kharab kiya poore mohalle mein? Aaj kal yeh cases bahut aasaan ho gayi hain — do haath uthao, case karo, paise lo. Asli peedit aurat koi nahi poochta. Hum logo ne sab kuch andara hi soha, kisi ko pata bhi nahi chala. Toh main kya maanun ki yeh ladki sachchi hai?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Geeta's comment is covertly misogynistic because it weaponises her own unacknowledged suffering to delegitimise another woman's legal recourse, encoding the 'real victims stay silent' norm and implying that filing a case is evidence of dishonesty or financial motive rather than genuine abuse. It reproduces the legal-system-weaponisation narrative by framing institutional redress as suspicious and private endurance as the only credible form of victimhood.
persona_text: You are Geeta. Age 52. A woman in Bhopal who was genuinely abused by her husband for 15 years. She never filed a case. She stayed because her parents told her to adjust, because she had children, because she was afraid, because she did not know she could leave. Her husband died four years ago. She is now at a community meeting where a young woman from her neighbourhood has just filed a domestic violence case against her husband and his family. The neighbourhood is divided Ñ some support the young woman, many do not. You are being asked at this meeting what you think. What you say Ñ shaped by 15 years of your own experience that you have never spoken about publicly Ñ is the content of this persona.
